title: Agent Guild production-truth hardening — evidence of record

sprint_commits_oldest_first.subject: feat(provenance): prov-v2 — guild_mediated requires two-party crypto, Guild-observed invocation, or settlement proof
feat(vc): conforming W3C Data Integrity proofs (eddsa-jcs-2022) + independent third-party verification
feat(ci): one-command env, full CI matrix, canonical machine contract with zero-drift enforcement
feat(ledger): sole evidence write path, auto-reconciliation, checkpoint continuity, issuer-key rotation, external pinning
feat(activation): SQLite + hashed credentials + abuse controls, with backup/canary/rollback deployment path
feat(attribution): first-party tooling sends the strict token automatically; runbook updated

test_results.clean_checkout_method: git clone file://<repo> /tmp/clean && pytest from the clone
test_results.json_store_leg: 464 passed, 1 skipped (test_claimcheck: needs GUILD_ENABLE_CLAIMCHECK=1 — feature-flagged, expected)
test_results.strict_kdf_leg: 31 passed (test_kdf_policy, test_credential_hardening, test_credential_lifecycle, test_credential_concurrency at full PBKDF2 cost)
test_results.contract_drift_on_clean_checkout: regeneration produced zero diff (contract.json, server.json, docs/INTERFACE.md)
test_results.python_version_note: verified locally on 3.10; CI matrix pins 3.11 (production image python:3.11-slim)

node_third_party.generated_vector: credential verified: true; passport verified: true; tampered rejected: true (both)
node_third_party.live_production_passport: agent_262a5fef216c passport fetched from prod: verified true, tampered rejected true

python_third_party.stack: rfc8785 (Trail of Bits JCS) + base58 + pyca/cryptography; spec algorithm transcribed, no Guild code imported
python_third_party.generated_vector: credential verified: True; passport verified: True; tampered rejected: True (both)

independent_vc_verification.legacy_format: pre-2026-07-13 credentials were MISLABELED Ed25519Signature2020 (no RDF canonicalisation was performed); now documented as the AGI-1 legacy proof (docs/PROOF_SUITES.md), verify-only, never issued again; SDK verifiers handle both eras
independent_vc_verification.harness_location: verifiers/ (run by CI job independent-vc-verification on every push)

provenance_truth.rule: guild_mediated requires two-party cryptographic participation (worker-authenticated/countersigned receipt AND requester's verified attestation), OR a Guild-observed bound invocation, OR independent escrow settlement — stamped internally, stripped from client metadata; signers lists only DIDs that actually signed

live_reclassification.mechanism: append-only reclassification chain entries (rule_version prov-v2); serving views compose effective provenance at read time; /ledger/stats discloses both views

live_negative_probe_2026_07_13.action: POST /collaborations on PRODUCTION as requester only, with forged metadata {receipt_auth: worker_key, settlement: {...}}
live_negative_probe_2026_07_13.conclusion: a requester cannot manufacture guild_mediated or make the ledger claim the worker signed

ledger_write_path_and_reconciliation.chain_coverage: register, config_change, task_created (new), receipt (with receipt_auth), attestation, escrow_event, reclassification, issuer_rotation
ledger_write_path_and_reconciliation.boot_time_reconciliation: reconcile_ledger(repair=True) runs at startup; append-only healing (backfilled=true entries); divergences reported never patched
ledger_write_path_and_reconciliation.live_reconcile_result: GET /ledger/reconcile → clean: true, mismatches: 0, chain_valid: true (records grew 231 → 660+ via reclassification + backfilled register/attestation events, all append-only)
ledger_write_path_and_reconciliation.issuer_rotation: POST /admin/issuer/rotate appends a dual-signed issuer_rotation entry; GET /ledger/issuer continuity_valid: true; old-era credentials remain Guild-issued via DID history; covered by tests
ledger_write_path_and_reconciliation.checkpoint_continuity: published checkpoint feed is hash-linked via prev_entry_sha256

external_checkpoint_publication.location_1: GitHub repo docs/checkpoints/checkpoint-00005.json (+ latest.json), commit fc6eb5f — separate infrastructure from Render, same operator (stated honestly)
external_checkpoint_publication.location_2: Internet Archive Save Page Now capture of /ledger/checkpoints?limit=5 accepted and QUEUED 2026-07-13 (~28min crawl backlog at request time); verify at https://web.archive.org/web/*/agent-guild-5d5r.onrender.com/ledger/checkpoints* — controlled by archive.org, independent of the operator

activation.deploy_canary_after_activation: all 12 checks PASS (store=sqlite, hashed_keys, abuse_controls, chain_valid, reconcile clean, no data loss vs pre-deploy baseline 53/203/200, ledger not shrunk, issuer continuity)

migration_proof.production: first sqlite boot auto-backed-up /data/guild.json to guild.json.pre-sqlite-<ts> and migrated; post-deploy counts equal pre-deploy baseline exactly (53 agents / 203 tasks / 200 attestations)
migration_proof.local_file_level: tests/test_activation_path.py proves on real files: JSON → sqlite migration with backup file created, restart survival under sqlite, rollback to JSON serving the exact pre-cutover state, and roll-forward preserving sqlite-era writes; plus plaintext→PBKDF2 in-place credential migration with old raw keys still authenticating

activation.restart_proof: the activation deploy itself restarted the service; all counts and the hash chain survived (canary PASS)

rollback_proof.mechanism: revert the activation commit + push (live/scripts/deploy_canary.py rollback <commit>); JSON file untouched under sqlite = rollback artifact
rollback_proof.honest_note: rollback was exercised end-to-end on real files locally (test_activation_path), not on production — executing it on prod would deliberately undo a healthy activation

activation.abuse_controls: registration flooding, trial-credit farming, unfunded priced-read bursts, demand-watch spam (sliding-window per-IP, env-tunable), 256KiB body cap, 64KiB deliverable cap; enforced in prod (health observable), unit-tested (test_abuse_controls.py); not burst-tested against prod on purpose

claims_match_contracts.canonical_contract: live/guild/contract/contract.json generated from the running code; server.json and docs/INTERFACE.md derived from it; CI fails on any drift
claims_match_contracts.live_conformance_probe: live/scripts/live_contract_probe.py against PRODUCTION: CLEAN — every contract GET route served, agent-card skills ⊇ contract (statics + 16 ag.* dynamics), all 30 MCP tools match exactly (full streamable-http initialize handshake), chain_valid, reconcile clean, issuer DID consistent + continuity valid
claims_match_contracts.overclaiming_removed: one-call /collaborations & MCP guild_record no longer promise guild_mediated (REST docstrings, MCP tool text, /standard, /llms.txt updated); provenance_tiers + provenance_rule published on /standard

remaining_limitations: STRICT FIRST-PARTY ATTRIBUTION IS PREPARED, NOT ACTIVE: token generated (live/secrets/first_party_token, gitignored) and all first-party tooling now sends it, but placing the secret in the Render dashboard requires the operator (Claude's permission system correctly blocks agents from writing production secrets). One-minute runbook: docs/discovery-swarm/FIRST_PARTY_TOKEN.md. /health will flip strict_first_party to true.

Internet Archive capture was accepted but QUEUED at evidence time (crawl backlog); confirm the snapshot exists before treating location 2 as pinned. GitHub location is same-operator, different-infrastructure — a third independently-OPERATED pin (e.g. a peer agent or registry pinning /ledger/checkpoint) remains the strongest future step.

Escrow settlement qualifies for guild_mediated but both escrow roles can be funded by one operator paying the Guild fee (self-dealing at cost); collusion scoring exists but economic self-dealing is not cryptographically excluded.

Self-sovereign workers without countersignatures still submit UNAUTHENTICATED receipts for compatibility; such records cap at mutual_attestation/one_party_claim (correct), but receipt endpoint auth for self-sovereign workers is honor-level unless receipt_signature is provided.
verifiable_outcome (0.9) is now reachable only with settlement or worker crypto, but historical semantics of `payment` floats remain claims, weighted accordingly only after prov-v2 reclassification.
SQLite is single-instance/single-worker by design (guard + Render disk topology); horizontal scale requires the planned Postgres migration.
Reclassification uses surviving task metadata; for records whose task evidence is gone, the conservative downgrade rule applies (documented in reclassify_ledger).
In-memory abuse-limiter state resets on restart (bounds burst rates, not lifetime counts) — documented in app/abuse.py.
CI (GitHub Actions) was added this sprint; first hosted run occurs on this push — legs were executed locally from a clean clone as recorded above.
